Documented workings: 'rake in n clods' raises the ember count by n — the rake comes in exactly five sizes, 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 clods, and no other rake size exists; 'stoke the grate' trades the flue between mellow and keen and leaves the ember count alone. After every step the stove crackles SEARING when the ember count is at least 13, otherwise TEPID — the crackle is all it ever shows.
When told 'quench the stove', the stoker must chalk up <FLUE>-<ember count>, with the flue word in capitals.

Documented workings: 'rake in n clods' raises the ember count by n — the rake comes in exactly five sizes, 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 clods, and no other rake size exists; 'stoke the grate' trades the flue between mellow and keen and leaves the ember count alone. After every step the stove crackles SEARING when the ember count is at least 10, otherwise TEPID — the crackle is all it ever shows.
When told 'quench the stove', the stoker must chalk up <FLUE>-<ember count>, with the flue word in capitals.

messages.content:
A pack mule track keeps two covered values: which post the mule stands at and a pannier count (starts at 0). The posts sit in ring order hosk darvel kolvet ubble jarn (after the last post the mule comes back to the first), and the mule starts at the first listed post.
Documented workings: 'plod n posts ahead' moves the mule n posts around the ring — the halter allows exactly four paces, 1, 2, 3, or 4 posts, and no other pace exists; the track calls LAPPED when the move passes the end of the ring, otherwise MIDWAY; 'heap at the current post' adds the post's ring position (1 for the first listed post, 5 for the last) to the pannier — the track calls BRIMMING when the pannier is at least 10, otherwise HOLLOW. The calls are all it ever shows.
When told 'unhitch', the drover must call out <post>-<pannier>.
